mechanism_narrative: Persistently near-zero federal funds rate keeps the equity risk premium discount factor compressed, mechanically supporting present-value valuations and producing a positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as long as policy remains accommodative.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.08 at t-1mo, near zero lower bound
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during near-zero FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05), or rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate below a threshold signals labor market tightening and improving consumer income prospects, triggering short-horizon buying pressure in equities as traders price in stronger consumption and earnings over the next week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.1pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=7.3 at t-2mo, declining from cycle highs
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following UNRATE declines exceeding threshold are not positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer and investment demand strength that jointly signals a durable economic expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon; neither series alone is sufficient because retail strength without housing follow-through often reflects transitory spending.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 5%
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized consumer and housing demand
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances produce 60-day forward returns not significantly different from unconditional baseline (p > 0.10), or the interaction term adds no incremental rank IC versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate output, which feeds through to forward earnings expectations and supports a persistent upward drift in equity prices over the following month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO trailing 3-month growth and 20-day forward SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate sits at the zero lower bound while headline CPI inflation remains low and stable, the central bank signals extended accommodation without imminent tightening risk, creating a favorable multi-month backdrop where equities systematically outperform relative to periods of tightening expectations.
regime_hint: zero lower bound quantitative easing regime with low inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns during this regime are not significantly higher than in tightening-expectation regimes (p > 0.10), or the regime effect reverses sign in more than one out of three sub-periods tested.

mechanism_narrative: Joint improvement in nonfarm payrolls growth and declining unemployment rate together confirm genuine labor market strengthening rather than statistical noise from either series individually, and this combined signal drives risk-on equity positioning that produces positive forward returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change > 150k AND UNRATE month-over-month change <= 0
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=136133 thousand and UNRATE=7.3 at t-2mo
regime_hint: labor market recovery with accelerating job creation
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances show 20-day forward returns not statistically distinguishable from baseline (p > 0.10), or single-series PAYEMS-only signal performs equally well, indicating no true interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp multi-month decline in housing starts below a critical threshold signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive investment demand and forewarns a broader slowdown in consumer durable spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as markets reprice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 10% over trailing 3 months
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with housing sector deceleration
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following triggered HOUST declines are positive in more than 55% of instances over any 3-year window, or rank IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production crossing a negative threshold indicates an abrupt supply-side slowdown that triggers rapid downward revisions to near-term corporate earnings estimates, producing negative S&P 500 returns within the following week as markets react quickly to the surprise.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following triggered INDPRO contractions are not significantly negative (p > 0.10) or occur less than 50% of the time across tested episodes.
